purpose: fixed current-main failure set for multi-file PDG / propagation continuation work

selection_basis: targets current bounded-frontier / propagation limits instead of older direct-boundary misses
shapes are small enough to become fixtures/regressions in later G11 tasks

defaults.note: keep engine-difference evaluation out of this set; this set isolates current PDG scope / propagation failure modes

cases.focus: caller result should recover a two-hop wrapper-return continuation across wrap -> step -> leaf

initial_source.leaf.rs: pub fn leaf(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

cases.why_it_matters: current propagation already handles direct boundary plus one-hop completion; this case fixes the next frontier where continuation needs two inter-file hops before it can explain the caller result

cases.focus: relevant second argument should survive a nested multi-input continuation through wrap -> pair

initial_source.pair.rs: pub fn pair(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 { b + 1 }

cases.why_it_matters: single-file two-arg FP guards already exist, but nested multi-input continuation is still weak; this case isolates the missing step without conflating it with direct-boundary scope selection

cases.focus: imported result should flow through wrapper-local alias chain and return to caller result

initial_source.value.rs: pub fn make(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

cases.why_it_matters: this is the smallest current-main case where return continuation and alias continuation interact; direct imported-result stitching works inside the wrapper, but it still does not naturally close back at the caller

cases.focus: require_relative split should recover a two-hop return continuation across wrap -> step -> leaf

initial_source.main.rb:
require_relative "lib/wrap"

def entry
  x = 1
  y = Wrap.wrap(x)
  puts y
end
initial_source.lib/wrap.rb:
require_relative "step"

module Wrap
  def self.wrap(a)
    Step.step(a)
  end
end
initial_source.lib/step.rb:
require_relative "leaf"

module Step
  def self.step(a)
    v = Leaf.leaf(a)
    v + 1
  end
end
initial_source.lib/leaf.rb:
module Leaf
  def self.leaf(a)
    a + 1
  end
end

cases.why_it_matters: direct Ruby require_relative success cases already exist, but this keeps the current frontier honest: Ruby still weakens once continuation must cross two file boundaries before returning to the caller

cases.focus: bounded slice planner should not drop a third semantically valid leaf only because per-seed tier2 budget is exhausted

initial_source.a_leaf.rs: pub fn leaf_a(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }
initial_source.b_leaf.rs: pub fn leaf_b(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 2 }
initial_source.c_leaf.rs: pub fn leaf_c(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 3 }

cases.why_it_matters: G11 is about bounded slices, not unbounded growth, but the current budget is still crude; this case fixes a concrete planner failure where the third valid leaf loses for purely budgetary reasons
